meta.name: FMI-14 Freiburg Mindfulness Inventory
meta.description: The FMI-14 is a 14-item self-report scale measuring mindfulness as a general human capacity. It captures the ability to be present and non-judgmental in everyday experiences. The scale is suitable for use with both meditators and non-meditators, assessing mindfulness as a trait that varies across individuals.
meta.author: Walach et al. (2006)
meta.reference: Walach H, Buchheld N, Buttenmuller V, Kleinknecht N, Schmidt S. Measuring mindfulness — the Freiburg Mindfulness Inventory (FMI). Personality and Individual Differences. 2006;40(8):1543-1555.
meta.instruction: Please rate each statement according to how frequently or infrequently you have had each experience over the last 14 days.

questions.text: I am open to the experience of the present moment.
I sense my body, whether eating, cooking, cleaning or talking.
When I notice an absence of mind, I gently return to the experience of the here and now.
I am able to appreciate myself.
I pay attention to what's behind my actions.
I see my mistakes and difficulties without judging them.
I feel connected to my experience in the here-and-now.
I am friendly to myself when things go wrong.
I watch my feelings without getting lost in them.
In difficult situations, I can pause without immediately reacting.
I experience moments of inner peace and ease, even when things get hectic and stressful.
I am impatient with myself and with others.
I am able to smile when I notice how I sometimes make life difficult.

ranges.label: Low Mindfulness
ranges.description: Your mindfulness level is low. You may often find yourself on autopilot, reacting impulsively to situations rather than responding with awareness. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Begin with short daily mindfulness exercises, such as 5 minutes of focused breathing
Practice bringing attention to routine activities like eating or walking
Consider joining a beginner mindfulness or meditation class
Try using a guided meditation app to build a regular practice
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible

ranges.label: Moderate Mindfulness
ranges.description: You have a moderate level of mindfulness. You are sometimes able to stay present and aware, but may still get caught up in automatic reactions at times.
suggestions: Increase the duration and consistency of your mindfulness practice
Practice observing your thoughts and emotions without judgment throughout the day
Try body scan exercises to deepen body awareness
Explore mindful communication in your relationships
Consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: High Mindfulness
ranges.description: You demonstrate a high level of mindfulness. You are generally able to stay present, accept experiences without judgment, and respond thoughtfully to challenges.
suggestions: Continue your mindfulness practice to maintain and deepen your skills
Explore advanced practices such as loving-kindness or insight meditation
Consider how your mindfulness can benefit others around you
Challenge yourself with mindfulness in more difficult situations
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Very High Mindfulness
ranges.description: You have a very high level of mindfulness. You consistently demonstrate present-moment awareness, non-judgmental acceptance, and equanimity in daily life.
suggestions: Maintain your strong mindfulness practice
Consider sharing your experience through teaching or mentoring others
Continue deepening your practice through retreats or advanced study
Use your mindfulness skills to support your overall wellbeing and relationships
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
